div1.entryFree:
Memorable, 1) kept in memory, remembered: witness our too much m. shame when Cressy battle fatally was struck, H5 II, 4, 53.
2) tending to preserve the remembrance of something, commemorative: he sends you this most m. line, in every branch truly demonstrative, H5 II, 4, 88. I wear it for a m. honour, IV, 7, 109. worn as a m. trophy of predeceased valour, V, 1, 76.
